paper_title: Towards a Unified Approach for Predicting the Range of Localization

methods.description: A semi-analytical method to predict the range of localization by considering the dependence on pulse amplitude for localizations 1 and 2, and using correction factors for localizations 3 and 4.

steps.description: Derive the unified parameter for the combination effect of localizations 1 and 2.
steps.searchable_summary: derive unified parameter for localizations 1 and 2 based on pulse amplitude

steps.description: Determine the correlation factors for localizations 3 and 4 through numerical evaluations for limited cases.
steps.searchable_summary: numerically evaluate correlation factors for localizations 3 and 4

methods.method_name: Full Analytical Approach for Localization Prediction
methods.description: A full analytical method using orthogonal polynomials to predict the asymptotic behavior of time evolution for localizations, aiming to extend predictions to all localizations without numerical simulations.

steps.description: Formulate the asymptotic behavior of time evolution using orthogonal polynomials.
steps.searchable_summary: formulate asymptotic behavior using orthogonal polynomials

steps.description: Extend the approach to localizations 1 and 2 for rotational excitation in diatomic molecules.
steps.searchable_summary: extend orthogonal polynomial approach to localizations 1 and 2

methods.method_name: Mathematical Model for Molecular Rotational Excitation
methods.description: A mathematical model describing molecular rotational excitation using a time-dependent Schrödinger equation, eliminating the assumption of constant transition probability.

steps.description: Define the time-dependent Schrödinger equation system for molecular rotational excitation.
steps.searchable_summary: define Schrödinger equation for rotational excitation

steps.description: Express a train of optical pulses as a summation of cosine waves.
steps.searchable_summary: express optical pulses as cosine wave summation

steps.description: Validate the correctness of the rotating-wave approximation by numerical calculations.
steps.searchable_summary: validate rotating-wave approximation with numerical calculations
